event: physical examination revealed erythematous changes over the trunk, from the chest to the abdomen
whole body computed tomography (CT) was performed
initial ER CT; no fluid located at the pericardium
fractures of the right third to fifth ribs
liver laceration with a minor active bleed
trans-arterial embolization of the right hepatic artery
admitted to the intensive care unit
10 French pigtail chest catheter was placed
no pleural effusion over the right costophrenic angle
drainage fluid was a moderate to dark red color
significant improvement in the hemithorax white-out
significant improvement in the fluid amount
transferred to a regular hospital ward
echo-guided pigtail insertion was subsequently performed
echo- and ultrasound-guided tube placement was performed
drainage fluid culture positive for Staphylococcus aureus
CT disclosed a severe purulent pericardial effusion at the right heart border
cardiovascular surgeon consulted for emergency surgery
compression of the right atrium and right ventricle by a massive yellow purulent exudate
around 500 mL of purulent exudate was removed
right atrium/superior vena cava (RA/SVC) junction tear was found
purulent exudate culture positive for Staphylococcus aureus
blood culture positive for Staphylococcus aureus
follow-up CT revealed bilateral lung pleural effusion and empyema
multiple bilateral loculated effusions and exudates were removed
